findings.description: search_by_name interpolates the user-supplied fragment directly into the SQL string, allowing SQL injection from the admin console.
Off-by-one: reserve uses stock > quantity instead of >=, so reserving exactly the available stock fails when it should succeed.
TOCTOU race: the SELECT stock and the subsequent UPDATE are not atomic, so concurrent reservations can oversell the same units.
The decrement UPDATE lacks a WHERE stock >= ? guard, so the conditional check is not enforced atomically at the database level.
low_stock_report issues an N+1 query, running a separate movements COUNT for every low-stock product instead of a single JOIN/GROUP BY.
restock does not validate quantity, so a negative value silently decrements stock and bypasses the reserve availability check.
reserve does not validate quantity, so a negative quantity passes the check and increases stock via stock - (negative).
restock performs an UPDATE on a possibly nonexistent sku and returns None, silently doing nothing with no caller feedback.
Product(**row) relies on the SELECT column set exactly matching the dataclass fields; any added/renamed column would raise a TypeError.
Every method opens and closes a fresh SQLite connection with no reuse, adding overhead under repeated calls.
The module-level logger is defined but never used anywhere in the file.
